S03.meta: // SLIDE 03 — THREE WAYS THE SYSTEM BREAKS

data.title: THREE WAYS THE SYSTEM BREAKS.
blocks: Production color value off by a shade — Very low — passes visual review
Button padding wrong by 2px — Low — within design rounding tolerance
Icon asset outdated (pre-redesign version) — Medium — obvious once spotted
Token name referenced in code no longer exists — High — build or runtime error

data.title: WHAT NEEDS TO HAPPEN INSTEAD.
blocks: The diagnosis leads directly to the prescription. If the Figma file is genuinely the source of truth — if that phrase is
What Needs to Happen Instead — Not a better naming convention. Not a more careful export checklist. Not a Confluence page documenting the correct proce

S05.meta: // SLIDE 05 — WHEN MANUAL EXPORT IS ACTUALLY

data.title: WHEN MANUAL EXPORT IS ACTUALLY FINE.
blocks: Team size — 2–4 people; communication overhead low
Platform count — Single platform, one codebase, one destination
Change frequency — Rare (twice a year or less); short risk window
Downstream automation — No build tool, code generator, or AI agent reads Figma data

S06.meta: // SLIDE 06 — THE CONCRETE BEFORE AND AFTER

data.title: THE CONCRETE BEFORE AND AFTER
blocks: Who performs the crossing — Designer or design-system engineer, manually
Output filename — Whatever the exporter named it (tokens_final_v3.json)
Validation — None enforced; errors visible only in production
Audit trail — Slack DM, email, or Git commit with human-provided message

data.title: WHAT I DON'T FULLY UNDERSTAND.
blocks: There is a thing about this problem I have not fully resolved, and I want to be honest about it.
